copy.title: Spurs steal Game 7. Cole leads at Sherwood. Market treads water.
copy.sportsAngle:
# Sports

The Spurs ended Oklahoma City's season in Game 7, with Tim Duncan's steady two-way play wearing down the Thunder down the stretch. San Antonio advances to the Finals after a grinding seven-game series that showed why Duncan remains the best power forward in basketball. Next up: the winner of the other conference final.

**Conversation starter:** Duncan's 24 points and 13 rebounds in a Game 7—at his age—is exactly why his Hall of Fame case is airtight.
copy.marketsTake:
**Markets**

Microsoft's 5.4% jump on enterprise AI wins proves the mega-cap rotation is real—Satya Nadella's getting the actual deals while Nvidia sweats margin pressure. The rest of the market's shrugging (SPY barely moved) because everyone's waiting to see if this is a sustainable shift or just noise before earnings season.

Watch the Magnificent Seven earnings next week—if Microsoft's upside translates to real guidance boosts, the AI money finally has a credible landing spot beyond chip stocks. Tesla and Nvidia's declines suggest some traders are taking "AI infrastructure" profits before the forward guidance gets tested.

*Bring this up: "Microsoft's running circles on Nvidia because they actually sell to paying customers."*
copy.golfNote: Eric Cole's -12 lead at Schwab is real; he's not giving it back to this field.
copy.sharpTake:
# SHARP TAKE

The Spurs just won a Game 7 on the road without their best player healthy—that's championship DNA. San Antonio's halfcourt defense choked OKC in crunch time, and Derrick White made every winning play when it mattered. They're heading to the Finals against Boston, and nobody should be surprised.

Markets stayed flat while you weren't looking. SPY inched up 0.2%, which means money is waiting for something—Fed speak, earnings revisions, or a real catalyst. Eric Cole leads the Schwab at -12 with 36 holes left, but that's a conversation for Sunday afternoon.

Saturday's clear. Tell your group chat the answer is no—you've got eighteen booked and a Finals matchup to catch later.
